intent: Warm-tint the shared app_icons family (charcoal field + accent) to match the unified-design-system palette rework, and fix the stale hex/prose left over from the accent-unification pass.
changes: style.py: BACKGROUND charcoal #1B1D21 -> #211D1B (byte-reversed mirror, same relative lightness)
apps.py: all 6 apps' accent #4C7DF0 -> #B8862E; docstring hex updated to match
test_registry.py: test_accents_are_shared assertion updated to the new accent
README.md: stale charcoal hex and 'own theme accent' wording (already false before this change) corrected

verification.evidence: Visually reviewed contact sheet: all 6 icons render coherently with warm charcoal + warm gold
Wrote assets + regenerated Android mipmaps for all 6 consuming repos; diet-guard's rebuilt APK pixel-verified on a physical device

risks: 5 of 6 consuming apps (all but diet-guard) had their icon source assets regenerated but their installed APKs were not rebuilt this session, so phone launcher icons for those apps remain stale until each is rebuilt/reinstalled separately
rollback: git revert this commit in testsAndMisc; each consuming repo's own icon-asset commit can be reverted independently since the generator only writes files, it doesn't couple repos together
After rollback, re-run 'python -m python_pkg.app_icons generate --android' to restore the old cool-palette assets everywhere
